meaning: n.中立状态,[军]压制,[化]中和(作用),[电]中和,平衡
examples: It was characterised by neutralisation test, hemagglutination inhibition test and complete sequencing of the genome.
Neutralisation. When an alkalic cleaning agent was used, the water is chemically neutralized in order to prevent corrosion.
In the case of a recognised accident, the rider shall be entitled to a neutralisation during the number of laps closest to 1300 metres.
The elimination or neutralisation of negative stereotypes would be part of that process.
An organisation called Meow Mitzvah is part of the search for a solution which will almost certainly involve a mass programme of spaying and neutralisation.
